description_long: This is a medium-sized, irregularly shaped stone with a predominantly white coloration and subtle grayish-green veining throughout its surface. The stone has a rough, jagged texture with uneven faces and a prominent peak on one side, giving it a natural, uncut appearance. One face displays a slightly more angular, flat surface, while the opposite side is more rugged and fractured. The edges are sharp in some areas and more rounded in others, suggesting minimal weathering. The stone's coloration and texture indicate it could be a type of marble or quartzite, commonly found in urban landscaping or as a decorative barrier. Its solid, dense structure makes it suitable for use as an obstacle or boundary marker in parks, sidewalks, or garden paths.
description: A rough, white stone with gray-green veining and an irregular, jagged shape. Its natural, uncut appearance and dense structure make it suitable as an urban barrier or decorative obstacle.
description_view_0: This view shows the stone's broad, flat face with a prominent peak and subtle gray veining.
description_view_1: This angle highlights the stone's angular edge and a rougher, more fractured side with visible texture.
description_view_2: This side reveals a smoother, less angular surface with gentle slopes and minimal veining.
description_view_3: This view presents the stone's most irregular face, with a pronounced peak and mottled gray-green patches.
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature or boundary marker
attribute_pattern: Subtle, irregular gray-green veining and mottling across a predominantly white surface, with no artificial carvings or regular patterns.
attribute_weathering: Slightly rough and unpolished, with sharp edges and minimal rounding, indicating limited exposure to weathering.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Contrasts with manicured lawns or paved surfaces, but can blend with other natural stones or vegetation when used in landscaping.
